name: org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin
description: GW Maven Plugin (Ghostwriter Maven Plugin) is a Maven plugin for AI-assisted documentation automation in Java/Maven projects using the MachAI Ghostwriter guided-workflow (GW) processors. It scans a documentation source tree (commonly src/site, configurable via gw.scanDir) for files containing embedded "@guidance:" blocks and runs transformations/synthesis through a configured GenAI provider/model (gw.model). The plugin provides goals: gw:gw (aggregator goal that can run without a pom.xml and processes modules in reverse order: sub-modules first, then parent modules, similar to the Ghostwriter CLI), gw:reactor (reactor-ordered processing using standard Maven reactor logic; typically scans from the execution root/basedir), gw:act (interactive or non-interactive action prompt applied across scanned documents via gw.act, optionally using an actions directory via gw.acts), gw:act-reactor (reactor-context variant that typically targets the execution-root project and may skip scanning when not in the right context), and gw:clean (deletes temporary artifacts created by GW processing). Common configuration parameters include gw.model, gw.scanDir, gw.instructions, gw.guidance, gw.excludes, logInputs, and for gw:gw an additional gw.threads toggle for multi-threaded module processing. GenAI credentials can be sourced from ~/.m2/settings.xml by setting -Dgenai.serverId=<serverId>; the plugin reads the <server> entry (username/password) and also forwards any custom <configuration> child elements as workflow properties. The plugin integrates with MachAI GenAI provider management (usage logging) and supports Maven parallel build concurrency by using Maven session degree-of-concurrency when Maven parallel mode is enabled.

constructors.description: Maven Mojo entry point for the gw:gw goal. Instantiated by Maven to run guided workflow document processing as an aggregator goal (requiresProject=false) with reverse module processing order (sub-modules first, then parent modules).

examples.snippet:
// Typical usage is from a shell:
// mvn gw:gw

features.name: gw:gw (aggregator guided workflow processing)
features.description: Runs Ghostwriter guided document processing as an aggregator Maven goal (requiresProject=false). Scans documents for embedded guidance blocks and processes modules in reverse order (sub-modules first, then parent), similar to the Ghostwriter CLI. Uses the configured model (gw.model), scan root (gw.scanDir; defaults to Maven execution root directory), instructions (gw.instructions), default guidance (gw.guidance), excludes (gw.excludes), and input logging (logInputs). If Maven parallel mode is enabled, it uses Maven's degree-of-concurrency when processing. Credentials can be sourced from ~/.m2/settings.xml via -Dgenai.serverId and forwarded as GENAI_USERNAME/GENAI_PASSWORD plus any custom server configuration properties. Also supports -Dgw.threads (goal-specific) to enable/disable multi-threaded module processing as documented in this project.

examples.description: Run guided processing in the current directory (can run without a pom.xml).
Run guided processing over src/site using a specific provider/model and log scanned inputs.
Run guided processing against a specific module.
Run guided processing and source GenAI credentials from ~/.m2/settings.xml.
Enable multi-threaded module processing (goal-specific parameter).

features.name: gw:reactor (reactor-ordered guided workflow processing)
features.description: Runs guided document processing in a standard Maven reactor build context. Uses Maven reactor project layout detection (ProjectLayoutManager) and binds the current MavenProject model into a MavenProjectLayout when applicable. Inherits common parameters from AbstractGWGoal (gw.model, gw.scanDir, gw.instructions, gw.guidance, gw.excludes, genai.serverId, logInputs). Defaults scanDir to the module basedir if not set and typically operates from the execution root directory for project layout. Credentials can be sourced from Maven settings.xml when genai.serverId is provided.

examples.description: Process projects using Maven reactor ordering.
Override the scan root directory.
Source GenAI credentials from Maven settings.xml.

features.name: gw:act (interactive action processing)
features.description: Runs an interactive (or provided) action prompt over scanned documents using ActProcessor. The action prompt is provided via -Dgw.act; if not provided, the goal prompts interactively via Maven Prompter and supports multi-line input using Ghostwriter.MULTIPLE_LINES_BREAKER. Optionally uses an actions directory via -Dgw.acts to source predefined action definitions. Supports excludes parsing from configuration (comma-separated string) and forwards common scanning parameters (gw.scanDir, gw.excludes, logInputs). Detects non-recursive invocation and uses Maven parallel degree-of-concurrency when enabled.

examples.description: Run and provide the action prompt interactively.
Run with a non-interactive action prompt against a specific scan directory.
Use an actions directory and log scanned input files.

features.name: gw:act-reactor (reactor-context action processing)
features.description: Reactor-context variant of gw:act. Applies or prompts for gw.act, detects project layout from the execution root (ProjectLayoutManager), and typically scans only when invoked for the execution-root project and not in a non-recursive context. Delegates action processing to ActProcessor, while overriding module processing to a no-op (reactor context).

examples.description: Run action processing in a reactor context (typically execution-root project).
Run with an explicit action prompt and scan directory override.

features.name: gw:clean (delete temporary GW artifacts)
features.description: Deletes temporary artifacts created by GW processing under the current Maven module base directory by calling org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor.deleteTempFiles(java.io.File). Typically bound to the Maven clean lifecycle phase.

examples.description: Remove GW temporary files for the current module.

features.name: Credentials from Maven settings.xml
features.description: Builds workflow configuration. When -Dgenai.serverId is set, reads ~/.m2/settings.xml <servers><server> credentials (username/password) and forwards them to the workflow as GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD. Additionally forwards any custom <configuration> child elements from the <server> entry as workflow properties.

examples.description: Source provider credentials from Maven settings.xml and forward them into the GW workflow.

customizations.name: Maven plugin parameters (system properties / plugin configuration)
customizations.description: The plugin is configured via Maven parameters and system properties including gw.model, gw.scanDir, gw.instructions, gw.guidance, gw.excludes, genai.serverId, and logInputs. Action goals additionally use gw.act and gw.acts. The gw:gw goal also supports gw.threads as a goal-specific toggle for multi-threaded module processing.

examples.description: Configure common scanning options via plugin configuration.

Run guided processing using a specific provider/model and scan directory.

Run action processing in a reactor context (typically execution-root project).
